title: Shang China practice
description: Practice your skills with a quiz on Shang China!

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage and answer the question. 

>The Son of Heaven [the emperor] mounts his jade carriage drawn by dragon-like horses, on which is displayed the great standard with the likenesses of the sun and moon upon it, along with dragons ascending and descending. In this the emperor goes to worship the sun outside the east gate. 

From: _Book of Etiquette and Ceremonial_, probably composed between the eighth and fifth centuries BCE. This book described rules and rituals related to Chinese royalty.  

**The description of the role of the ruler given in this passage _differed_ most strongly from rulers in which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Classical Athens
Achaemenid Persia
Ancient Egypt
Ancient Mesopotamia

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage and answer the question. 

>The Son of Heaven [the emperor] mounts his jade carriage drawn by dragon-like horses, on which is displayed the great standard with the likenesses of the sun and moon upon it, along with dragons ascending and descending. In this the emperor goes to worship the sun outside the east gate. 

From: _Book of Etiquette and Ceremonial_, probably composed between the eighth and fifth centuries BCE. This book described rules and rituals related to Chinese royalty.  

**This passage could best be used as evidence for the existence of which of following in ancient China?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The idea of divine kingship
Codification of monotheistic religious beliefs
Technological advances in transportation
Advanced astronomical knowledge

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following contextual factors bests explains the presence of chariots in Shang China?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Shang Dynasty burial with chariot and horses.
options.caption: Shang Dynasty burial with chariot and horses. Image credit: [thehistoryblog.com](http://www.thehistoryblog.com/archives/12651)

choices.content: Conflict and interactions with pastoral peoples spread military technology
The Chinese independently invented the chariot for agricultural use
Urbanization led to the need for smaller modes of transportation
Increased trade led to the invention of chariots to haul goods

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following was an effect of the introduction to China of chariots?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Shang Dynasty burial with chariot and horses.
options.caption: Shang Dynasty burial with chariot and horses. Image credit: [thehistoryblog.com](http://www.thehistoryblog.com/archives/12651)

choices.content: Military capability increased due to new technology
Trade increased due to larger, more efficient transportation
Social hierarchy increased as elites used new transportation
Ancestor veneration began through the use of grave goods

assessment_items.question_content:
Excerpt from oracle bone inscriptions of the late Shang Dynasty divined on behalf of the king, Wu Ding.
 
> Crack‑making  on  yiwei  (day  32),  Gu  divined:  “Father  Yi  (the  twentieth  Shang  king,  Xiao  Yi,  the  father  of  Wu  Ding)  is  harming  the  king.”

>  Grandfather Ding (the fifteenth king, father of Xiao Yi) is harming the king.

> It is not Grandfather Ding who is harming the king.

**A historian would most likely use these oracle bone inscriptions as evidence to argue that Shang China elites practiced which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Animism
Ancestor veneration
Vedic religion
Divine kingship

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**The image above could best be used as evidence for which of the following claims about urban planning in Shang China?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Excavated canal at Anyang, capital city of the late Shang dynasty.
options.caption: Excavated canal at Anyang, capital city of the late Shang dynasty. Image credit: [xinhuanet.com](http://news.xinhuanet.com/english/2016-07/21/c_135530934.htm)

choices.content: It was made possible by centralized government
It was required by legal codes in capital cities
It spread with interregional trade
It resulted in social hierarchy

assessment_items.question_content:
Refer to the image below.

[[☃ image 1]]

**This archaeological artifact can best be used as evidence for which of the following uses for writing in early civilizations?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: The image above shows an engraved ox shoulder blade discovered in ruins of the Shang Dynasty (1600 - 1046 B.C.) in China.
options.caption: The image above shows an engraved ox shoulder blade discovered in ruins of the Shang Dynasty (1600 - 1046 B.C.) in China. Image credit: [Wikimedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oracle_bone#/media/File:Shang_dynasty_inscribed_scapula.jpg)

choices.content: Writing for record keeping to facilitate trade
Writing for legal codes to promote social order
Writing to standardize vocations and labor specialization
Writing for ceremonial and ritual purposes

assessment_items.question_content:
Excerpt from oracle bone inscriptions of the late Shang Dynasty divined on behalf of the king, Wu Ding.
 
> Crack‑making  on  yiwei  (day 32),  Gu  divined:  “Father  Yi  (the  twentieth  Shang  king,  Xiao  Yi,  the  father  of  Wu  Ding)  is  harming  the  king.”

>  Grandfather Ding (the fifteenth king, father of Xiao Yi) is harming the king.	 

> It is not Grandfather Ding who is harming the king.

**A historian would most likely use these oracle bone inscriptions as evidence to study which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Interregional trade between China and India
Political dynamics among the Shang elites
Daily life among ordinary residents of the Shang dynasty
How Shang elites made decisions about raising livestock

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage and answer the question. 

>The things laid in the court are those produced in the state to which the noble belongs. The emperor carries in his hands the rolls of silk, and leads a team of horses . . . The horses are set in the center of the court . . . [the emperor gives the silk to the noble] then the noble lays down the silks and kowtows [bows to show respect] twice. 

From: _Book of Etiquette and Ceremonial_, probably composed between the eighth and fifth centuries BCE. This passage describes what happened when a nobleman visits the emperor. 

**This passage could best be used as evidence for which of the following claims about social hierarchy in ancient China?** 

 [[☃ radio 2]]

choices.content: It was reinforced by public ritual
It was connected to trade
It was tied to religious status
It was based on military power

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage and answer the question. 

>The things laid in the court are those produced in the state to which the noble belongs. The emperor carries in his hands the rolls of silk, and leads a team of horses . . . The horses are set in the center of the court . . . [the emperor gives the silk to the noble] then the noble lays down the silks and kowtows [bows to show respect] twice. 

From: _Book of Etiquette and Ceremonial_, probably composed between the eighth and fifth centuries BCE. This passage describes what happened when a nobleman visits the emperor. 

**The actions described in the passage are best understood in the context of which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The need of rulers to maintain social hierarchies
The reliance of governments on surplus agriculture
The importance of trade along the Silk Road
The introduction of chariots to China

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**This image best represents which of the following historical continuities in ancient China?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Grave of a Chinese noblewoman, Fu Hao, from the Shang period.
options.caption: Grave of a Chinese noblewoman, Fu Hao, from the Shang period. Image credit: [Wikimedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shang_dynasty#/media/File:Tomb_Fu_Hao_YinXu.jpg)

choices.content: Trade with other regions for grave goods
Social hierarchy in the form of patriarchy
Religious practice of Vedic beliefs
Ancestor veneration via grave goods
